summary:
## Metadata
- File path: .diffdocrc.example
- File name: .diffdocrc.example
- Extension: .example
- Inferred language/type: JSON configuration example file
- Content hash: f4f0176776ed7dd10d839be4e11ea210
- Line count: 20
- Byte size: 664
- Summary format: structured-functional-v1
- Notable symbols/classes/functions: $schema, baseDir, repoPath, aiProvider, localLlmEndpoint, localEmbedEndpoint, localChatModel, localEmbedModel, cloudLlmEndpoint, cloudChatModel, cloudEmbedModel, embedBatchSize, summarizeConcurrency, openaiApiKey, includeGlobs, excludeGlobs, ignoreFile
- External dependencies: JSON Schema at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sullyTheDev/diffdoc/main/schemas/v2/diffdocrc.schema.json; local Ollama-compatible API endpoints on http://localhost:11434/v1; OpenAI-compatible API endpoint on https://api.openai.com/v1
- Internal dependencies: None identified.
- Public API/exports: None identified.

## Purpose
This file provides an example DiffDoc configuration. It defines the settings needed to run the tool, including repository paths, AI provider selection, model endpoints, embedding/summarization concurrency, file filtering, and ignore-file location.

## User-Visible Behavior
When used as a configuration file, it directs DiffDoc to:
- store working data under `./.diffdoc`
- treat the current repository root as `.` 
- use the local AI provider by default
- connect to local model and embedding services on `localhost:11434`
- optionally support cloud/OpenAI-compatible endpoints and models
- process embeddings in batches of 25
- run summarization with concurrency of 2
- apply include/exclude glob filters if configured
- read ignore rules from `.diffdocignore`

## Business Rules
- The configuration is schema-driven via the referenced JSON Schema.
- The default AI provider is `local`.
- Local and cloud model settings are both present, but the active provider is controlled by `aiProvider`.
- Embedding and summarization throughput are constrained by `embedBatchSize` and `summarizeConcurrency`.
- File selection can be constrained by `includeGlobs` and `excludeGlobs`.
- Ignore rules are sourced from `.diffdocignore`.
- `openaiApiKey` is present but empty in the example, implying it must be supplied for cloud usage.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
Inputs:
- Configuration values in JSON format
- Repository-relative paths and glob patterns
- Local and cloud endpoint URLs
- Model names for chat and embeddings
- Optional OpenAI API key

Outputs:
- A configuration object consumed by DiffDoc
- Derived runtime behavior for indexing, embedding, and summarization
- Working directory/artifact location under `./.diffdoc`

## Side Effects
- Directs DiffDoc to read and write artifacts under `./.diffdoc`
- Causes network requests to configured local or cloud AI endpoints when the tool runs
- Causes DiffDoc to read `.diffdocignore` for exclusion rules

## Error And Edge Cases
- If the schema is unavailable or incompatible, configuration validation may fail.
- If local services are not running at the configured endpoints, AI operations may fail.
- If `openaiApiKey` remains empty while cloud mode is used, cloud requests may be unauthorized.
- Empty `includeGlobs` and `excludeGlobs` mean no explicit glob-based filtering is configured.
- Misconfigured model names or endpoints can prevent embedding or summarization from succeeding.

## Dependencies
- JSON Schema validation against the referenced schema URL
- Local Ollama-compatible service at `http://localhost:11434/v1`
- OpenAI-compatible API at `https://api.openai.com/v1`
- DiffDoc runtime support for `.diffdocignore`
- DiffDoc configuration parser/loader

## Operational Notes
- This is an example file, so values are intended as defaults or templates rather than guaranteed production settings.
- The local provider settings suggest the tool is optimized for self-hosted inference by default.
- Concurrency and batch size are small, which may reduce resource usage but limit throughput.
- The configuration is likely sensitive to endpoint availability and model naming; verify both before running.
- Because the file is schema-backed, changes should remain compatible with the referenced schema version.
